step_by_step_thinking: The question asks for the probability of a test detecting a truly positive person — i.e., correctly identifying someone who has the disease. This directly corresponds to **sensitivity**, defined as the proportion of true positives among those with the disease. Sensitivity measures the test's ability to identify individuals who actually have the condition, unlike specificity (which applies to healthy individuals) or predictive values (which depend on prevalence).
raw_response:
The question asks for the probability of a test detecting a truly positive person — i.e., correctly identifying someone who has the disease. This directly corresponds to **sensitivity**, defined as the proportion of true positives among those with the disease. Sensitivity measures the test's ability to identify individuals who actually have the condition, unlike specificity (which applies to healthy individuals) or predictive values (which depend on prevalence).
\boxed{C}
